concerns.analysis: Several meaningful charts and graphs from the source PDF are missing from the HTML output. Instead of being rendered as image elements (``), they appear only as text placeholders with descriptions (e.g., `[Image: Line chart...]`). This affects Figures 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 21, 22, and 23. These are critical data visualizations whose visual data is completely lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Figure 14 on page 56 is a line chart showing projections of SSB related to fishing at a level to end overfishing.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains a text placeholder instead of an image element: `[Image: Line chart showing projections of Spawning Stock Biomass (SSB) from 2018 to 2050 under different fishing scenarios...]`

concerns.analysis: Most lists in the document are extracted correctly, including nested lists. However, a major list flattening defect occurs for the list crossing pages 14 and 15. In the source PDF, the parent bullet 'From Supplement A to the Southern Flounder FMP Amendment 1...' contains three dashed sub-bullets ('A commercial minimum size limit...', 'A minimum mesh size of 6.0-ISM...', 'A minimum mesh size of 5.75-ISM...'). The conversion failed to nest these properly; instead, it extracted the sub-bullets as t
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Bullet: "From Supplement A to the Southern Flounder FMP Amendment 1, as modified by the Aug. 17, 2017 settlement agreement:" followed by indented dashed sub-items: "- A commercial minimum size limit of 15 inches TL;", "- A minimum mesh size of 6.0-ISM to harvest southern flounder from a gill net; and", "- A minimum mesh size of 5.75-ISM for pound net escape panels."
concerns.output_quote: The JSON array places these text strings as independent top-level items in the list array: `{"text": "From Supplement A..."}, {"text": "A commercial minimum size..."}, {"text": "A minimum mesh size..."}, {"text": "A minimum mesh size..."}`, failing to use the `children` key to nest them.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 4 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=4. Examples: pages 37-38: 9 cols (, season start date...); pages 46-47: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf...); pages 47-48: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 5 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=5, examples=['https://www.implan.com', 'http://www.implan.com/', 'mailto:Michael.loeffler@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (2 instance(s)): p21→p22: ordered list D→E continues; p22→p23: ordered list D→E continues
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=2. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "A. Trip Limits"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Year"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=0. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 15 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (15 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p50: "...s, overwhelmingly dominated by North Carolina at 9"; p50: "...s, overwhelmingly dominated by North Carolina at 9"; p51: "...tegories labeled with state abbreviations and "com"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=15 (truncated=15, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF uses underline emphasis for multiple elements. In most cases throughout the document, underlined text is correctly translated into structural headings, preserving emphasis. However, on page 27, the underlined text 'Advisory Committee Recommendations' and 'Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee' were extracted as paragraphs and lost their underline emphasis (only markdown italics were applied, which does not replace the missing underline).
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Advisory Committee Recommendations (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)

Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee (Both underlined and italicized)
concerns.output_quote:
*Advisory Committee Recommendations* (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)

*Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee*

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 2 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=2. Examples: p50: "A pie chart showing data distribution across four "; p55: "Line graph showing estimated fishing mortality rat". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.
header_footer_count=61. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
